mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and household spending power, which drives aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS growth show S&P 500 20-day forward returns that are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, the policy stance becomes restrictive enough to raise corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and contraction in industrial production indicates a demand-supply imbalance where consumption outpaces productive capacity, squeezing corporate margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During joint positive RSAFS and negative INDPRO regimes, the average S&P 500 60-day forward return is not negative (mean return >= 0%) or the Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the central bank to hike rates more aggressively than discounted by markets, raising the equity risk premium and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening regimes, top-quartile month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth does not produce significantly negative average S&P 500 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp three-month increase in industrial production above 1.5% signals accelerating manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises more than 1.5% over three months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of triggered observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 5% over three months signals weakening residential construction activity and reduced downstream demand for building materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After three-month HOUST declines exceeding 5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances in out-of-sample data.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and decelerating payroll growth produce a stagflationary environment where real wage growth turns negative and input costs rise simultaneously, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL rises and PAYEMS growth slows, the average S&P 500 20-day forward return is greater than or equal to zero, or the Rank IC between the interaction signal and returns is positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects broad-based expansion in aggregate demand and corporate revenues, which translates into positive earnings revisions and upward pressure on the S&P 500 over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive real GDPC1 growth are followed by negative average S&P 500 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
